name: Monte de la Mola y Castillo de la Olivella — circular por el Parque Natural del Garraf
region_intro: Jogging routes around Olivella traverse the diverse landscapes of Garraf Natural Park in Catalonia, Spain. The region features rolling hills, dense forests, and Mediterranean shrubland, providing varied terrain for runners. From tranquil paths to more challenging inclines, the area offers a range of environments for physical activity. Best jogging routes around Olivella The most popular jogging route is Cueva de Cal Muntaner y fuente del Rector — circular desde Olivella, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes about 45 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging experience through varied natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local runners is Monte de la Mola y Castillo de la Olivella — circular por el Parque Natural del Garraf, a difficult 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This route provides panoramic views of the Garraf Massif and includes historical landmarks. Local runners also love the Olivella Village – Forn del Monàs loop from Mas Milà, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Jogging around Olivella is defined by its integration with Garraf Natural Park, featuring rolling hills, dense forests, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult trails. The routes in Olivella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 4000 runners have used komoot to explore Olivella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Olivella?
A: Olivella offers a selection of 11 running routes, providing diverse options for outdoor enthusiasts. These include 4 moderate trails and 7 more challenging routes, ensuring there's something for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails in Olivella?
A: The running trails in Olivella traverse the beautiful and varied landscapes of the Garraf Natural Park. You can expect picturesque rolling hills, dense forests, and Mediterranean shrubland. Many routes also offer spectacular panoramic views of the Garraf Massif and the Barcelona coastline, with some passing by vineyards and the scenic Foix Reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Olivella?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Olivella are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130707959/cueva-de-cal-muntaner-y-fuente-del-rector-circular-desde-olivella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Cal Muntaner y fuente del Rector — circular desde Olivella&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 3.9-mile trail that takes you through varied natural surroundings.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Olivella?
A: The running trails in Olivella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful natural surroundings of Garraf Natural Park, and the rewarding panoramic views found on many routes.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites or landmarks?
A: Yes, several routes offer glimpses of historical sites and natural landmarks. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130723894/monte-de-la-mola-y-castillo-de-la-olivella-circular-por-el-parque-natural-del-garraf" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de la Mola y Castillo de la Olivella — circular por el Parque Natural del Garraf&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views and includes historical landmarks. You might also encounter views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle&lt;/a&gt; near the Foix Reservoir, or the unique architecture of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Buddhist Monastery&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes with good viewpoints for jogging in Olivella?
A: Absolutely. Olivella is known for its spectacular viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130723894/monte-de-la-mola-y-castillo-de-la-olivella-circular-por-el-parque-natural-del-garraf" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de la Mola y Castillo de la Olivella — circular por el Parque Natural del Garraf&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, offers extensive panoramic views of the Garraf Massif. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2724790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morella Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the second highest peak in the Garraf Massif, is another rewarding destination for breathtaking 360-degree vistas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging routes in Olivella?
A: While many routes in Olivella are rated moderate to difficult, active families might enjoy some of the moderate trails. The region's diverse landscapes offer opportunities for shorter, less strenuous sections within larger routes, especially those through forests or along flatter sections near the Foix Reservoir. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it suits your family's fitness level.

Q: Are there any running trails that lead to caves or unique natural formations?
A: Yes, the area around Olivella features several interesting caves and natural formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130707959/cueva-de-cal-muntaner-y-fuente-del-rector-circular-desde-olivella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Cal Muntaner y fuente del Rector — circular desde Olivella&lt;/a&gt; route specifically highlights a cave. Other notable caves in the vicinity include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Muntanyeta&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3843406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de Cal Muntaner&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Olivella?
A: As Olivella is located within the Garraf Natural Park, dogs are generally permitted on trails, but it's important to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other park users. Always check specific park regulations or signage for any restricted areas.

Q: Are there longer distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Olivella offers several longer and more challenging routes suitable for experienced runners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19099890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Grau Technical Trail – Cova de Cal Muntaner loop from Mas Milà&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.7-mile (23.6 km) route with significant elevation gain, perfect for those seeking a more demanding run.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for jogging in Olivella?
A: The terrain for jogging in Olivella is quite varied, reflecting its location within Garraf Natural Park. You'll encounter a mix of dirt paths, rocky sections, and some paved stretches, often winding through forests and Mediterranean shrubland. Expect rolling hills, offering both challenging inclines and gentler sections, providing a dynamic running experience.
tips: Puig de la Mola is a mountain in the Garraf massif, Catalonia, Spain. It has an elevation of 533 meters above sea level. It is located between the municipalities of Avinyonet del Penedès, Olesa de Bonesvalls, Begues and Olivella
At the top of this hill called Puig Molí is what is called Olivella Castle, a hill of more than 300 meters and from where you can see good views of the area.
Well-known source around the area where there is also a trough. From the same source you can see Olivella Castle.

waypoints.name: Puig de la Mola summit (534 m)
